alternate_names: 国家互联网信息办公室
notes: China's primary internet and cyberspace regulator. Responsible for AI regulation, data security, content moderation, and cybersecurity oversight. Part of the Central Cyberspace Affairs Commission.

match_nodes.description: Interim Measures for the Management of Generative Artificial Intelligence Services

data.official_name: Interim Measures for the Management of Generative Artificial Intelligence Services
alternate_names: 生成式人工智能服务管理暂行办法
data.description: Regulates generative AI services in China. Requires security assessments, content moderation, and data compliance.
data.notes: Effective August 15, 2023. Jointly issued by CAC, NDRC, Ministry of Education, MOST, MIIT, MPS, and SARFT. Requires providers to: conduct security assessments before public launch, implement content moderation, ensure training data legality, protect user privacy, and prevent generation of content that subverts state power or promotes terrorism. Applies to services offered to the public within China.

data.official_name: Personal Information Protection Law of the People's Republic of China
alternate_names: 中华人民共和国个人信息保护法
data.description: China's comprehensive personal information protection law (effective November 2021). Imposes GDPR-like obligations including consent, data minimisation, cross-border transfer restrictions, and DPO requirements.
data.notes: Companion to the Cybersecurity Law (CSL 2017) and Data Security Law (DSL 2021) — together form China's data-governance trinity.

data.official_name: Data Security Law of the People's Republic of China
alternate_names: 中华人民共和国数据安全法
data.description: China's data security law establishing tiered data classification (important data, core data), security obligations across the data lifecycle, and cross-border transfer restrictions for industrial / non-personal data. Effective September 2021.
data.notes: Operates alongside PIPL (personal information) and CSL (cybersecurity).

data.official_name: Cybersecurity Law of the People's Republic of China
alternate_names: 中华人民共和国网络安全法
data.description: China's foundational cybersecurity law (effective June 2017). Establishes the Multi-Level Protection Scheme (MLPS, operationalised via GB/T 22239), critical information infrastructure (CII) designation, network operator obligations, and cross-border data transfer constraints.
data.notes: Foundation for the broader Chinese cyber-governance framework.

data.description: Establishes a mandatory pre-clearance security-review regime for critical information infrastructure operators (CIIOs) procuring network products/services that could affect national security, and for network platform operators holding personal data of over 1 million users seeking a listing abroad. Reviews conducted by the Cybersecurity Review Office under CAC, jointly with 12 other departments (NDRC, MIIT, MPS, MSS, MOF, MOFCOM, PBOC, SAMR, and others).
data.notes: First issued 2020, revised 2021. Current version effective February 15, 2022.

urls.note: China Law Translate English translation, 2022 revised version

match_nodes.description: Measures for Security Assessment of Outbound Data Transfer

data.official_name: Measures for Security Assessment of Outbound Data Transfer
alternate_names: 数据出境安全评估办法
data.description: Implements PIPL Article 40 and DSL cross-border provisions. Requires a CAC security assessment before export of 'important data' or personal information above defined thresholds (e.g. CIIOs, processors handling PI of 1M+ individuals, or sensitive PI of 10,000+ individuals) collected/generated in China. Companion lower-threshold mechanisms (standard contract, certification) were introduced in later 2023 relaxation rules.
data.notes: Effective September 1, 2022, with a compliance grace period to March 1, 2023.

data.official_name: Provisions on the Administration of Algorithm Recommendation for Internet Information Services
alternate_names: 互联网信息服务算法推荐管理规定
data.description: China's first dedicated algorithm-governance regulation, grounded in the CSL, DSL, and PIPL. Regulates recommendation-algorithm use in internet information services (search, feeds, e-commerce, gig platforms): requires algorithm filing/registration with CAC for services with public-opinion or social-mobilization capability, mandates user opt-out/control over recommendation profiling, bans algorithmic price discrimination, and requires labor-rights protections for algorithm-managed gig workers. Jointly issued by CAC, MIIT, MPS, and SAMR.

data.official_name: Provisions on the Administration of Deep Synthesis of Internet-Based Information Services
alternate_names: 互联网信息服务深度合成管理规定
data.description: Regulates 'deep synthesis' technology (deep learning/VR/generative-synthetic algorithms producing text, images, audio, video, or virtual scenes/persons). Requires provider security assessments, real-name verification of users, conspicuous labeling of synthetic content (predecessor to the 2025 AI-content labeling measures below), bans use for spreading disinformation or impersonation without consent, and requires algorithm filing with CAC similar to the Algorithm Recommendation Provisions. Jointly issued by CAC, MIIT, and MPS.

match_nodes.description: Measures for Labeling of AI-Generated Synthetic Content

data.official_name: Measures for the Labeling of AI-Generated and Synthetic Content
alternate_names: 人工智能生成合成内容标识办法
data.description: Requires 'explicit' (visible text/audio/graphic) and 'implicit' (embedded metadata) labeling of AI-generated/synthetic content — text, image, audio, video, virtual scenes — by internet information service providers and content-distribution platforms. Paired with mandatory national standard GB 45438-2025 (Cybersecurity Technology — Labeling Method for Content Generated by Artificial Intelligence). Jointly issued by CAC, MPS, SAMR, and NRTA (National Radio and Television Administration).
